message.content:
You are analyzing naming convention patterns for a prefix-based naming system. Read and analyze:

`/Users/bhyslop/projects/cnmp_CellNodeMessagePrototype/lenses/wrs-WRC-WardRealmConcepts.adoc`

For your analysis, identify:
- All category prefixes declared in the mapping section (look for `// prefix_: Description`)
- The purpose of this document (what domain does "wrs" / "Ward Realm" cover?)
- Anchor↔attribute alignment patterns
- Count of attribute references
- Whether this follows MCM patterns (Strachey brackets, linked terms)
- Any cross-references to other specs

Format your response as a concise structured assessment suitable for adding to a memo about naming conventions.

   289→== Core Principles
   290→
   291→{smc_wardrealmsubstrate}
   292→provides a substrate for building domain-specific distributed state machines capable of meeting real-time constraints, not an opinionated framework with built-in reliability patterns.
   293→The system enforces mechanism while leaving all policy decisions to the business context, including critical resource management and scheduling decisions required for deterministic timing.
   294→
   295→State progression occurs exclusively through
   296→{mcd_message}
   297→exchange and
   298→{smc_timer}
   299→expiration, with no hidden communication channels or implicit coordination.
   300→All fault tolerance, consensus mechanisms, reliability patterns, and crucially, all resource management strategies emerge from the
   301→{smc_ward}
   302→behaviors you design, not from framework-provided abstractions.
   303→
   304→The architecture maintains a strict separation between
   305→{pmp_specification_context}
   306→definition,
   307→{pmp_transport_context}
   308→machinery, and
   309→{pmp_business_context}
   310→logic.
   311→Transport handles message serialization, routing, and the
   312→{pmp_executive_cycle},
   313→while business context determines all state transitions, failure responses, and resource allocation decisions.
   314→This separation enables the same
   315→{smc_accord}
   316→to run in radically different deployment configurations without modification, with
   317→{smc_constant_s}
   318→parameterizing all
   319→{ftc_bound_s}
   320→to control array sizing across deployments.
   321→
   322→{smc_wardrealmsubstrate}
   323→explicitly rejects framework-level fairness guarantees, queue depth management, or automatic backpressure mechanisms.
   324→When timer deadlines conflict with message processing capacity, business logic must implement appropriate shedding, prioritization, or failure strategies.
   325→This design philosophy enables systems that achieve 100% capability within their designed operational envelope rather than degrading gracefully across all possible load conditions.
   326→
   327→The system makes minimal assumptions about deployment topology, allowing
   328→{smc_shape_s}
   329→to define everything from single-threaded test harnesses to massively concurrent production deployments.
   330→By treating distribution as a deployment concern rather than a design constraint,
   331→{smc_wardrealmsubstrate}
   332→enables sophisticated testing through the
   333→{smc_forge}
   334→while maintaining production fidelity.
   335→
   336→Cross
   337→{pmp_realm} {mcd_message}
   338→ordering is explicitly undefined, requiring
   339→{smc_ward}
   340→designers to implement appropriate sequence handling, idempotency, or convergence strategies when
   341→{smc_ward_s}
   342→communicate across
   343→{pmp_realm}
   344→boundaries.
   345→No framework-level guarantees exist for message delivery,
   346→{smc_ward}
   347→liveness detection, or partition tolerance - these concerns belong entirely to the
   348→{pmp_business_context}
   349→implementation.
   350→
   351→{smc_timer}
   352→precision varies by deployment context, from microsecond-level precision in interrupt-driven
   353→{pmp_realm_s}
   354→to millisecond precision in standard operating system threads.
   355→
   356→== {csc_mcm_task_lens} Support
   357→
   358→{smc_wardrealmsubstrate}
   359→supports focused work contexts through
   360→{csc_mcm_task_lens_s}
   361→that emphasize relevant concepts while suppressing distractions.
   362→Each
   363→{csc_mcm_task_lens}
   364→creates an activation pattern optimizing capacity for specific
   365→{smc_accord}
   366→design or implementation tasks.
   367→See the
   368→{csc_tlg_task_lens_guide}
   369→(`csx-TLG-TaskLensGuide.md`) for structure and usage patterns.
   370→See the
   371→{csc_m2c_model_to_claudex}
   372→(`mcm-M2C-ModelToClaudex.md`) for mechanical compilation of
   373→{csc_mcm_task_lens_s}.
   374→
   375→== {smc_architecture_patterns}
   376→
   377→=== Topology and Transport Interaction
   378→
   379→Each
   380→{smc_shape}
   381→combines a
   382→{smc_topology}
   383→pattern with
   384→{smc_transport}
   385→mechanism selections to define how
   386→{mcd_message_s}
   387→flow through a
   388→{smc_alliance}.
   389→The
   390→{smc_topology}
   391→establishes the logical connection structure while
   392→{smc_transport}
   393→mechanisms implement the physical delivery.
   394→The
   395→{smc_shape}
   396→generates multiple
   397→{smc_fixture_s}
   398→each supporting different execution combinations.
   399→
   400→=== Cohort Fief Parallel Processing
   401→
   402→{smc_cohort_fief_s}
   403→extend the execution model by maintaining
   404→{smc_fief}-ledgers
   405→that process with higher priority than the
   406→{pmp_realm_p}
   407→ledger.
   408→This enables massively parallel
   409→{smc_ward}
   410→instance execution within a single
   411→{smc_fief}
   412→instance while preserving
   413→{smc_message}
   414→ordering guarantees at
   415→{smc_fief}
   416→boundaries.
   417→
   418→The scatter-gather pattern leverages three mechanisms:
   419→
   420→* {mcd_summons}:
   421→{mcd_phase_s}
   422→marked for
   423→{mcd_summons}
   424→are permitted to send simultaneously to all
   425→{smc_retinue}
   426→members
   427→* {gcm_glean} {gcm_handler_s}:
   428→Restricted
   429→{gcm_handler_s}
   430→that only read
   431→{smc_ward}
   432→properties and write to per-connection
   433→{smc_harvest_s},
   434→enabling safe parallel execution
   435→* {gcm_thresh} {gcm_handler_s}:
   436→{pmp_transport_context}
   437→invokes these after all
   438→{gcm_glean_s}
   439→complete, providing gathered results to the originating
   440→{smc_ward}
   441→instance.
   442→
   443→{smc_retinue} membership
   444→locks at first
   445→{gcm_sender}
   446→invocation within a
   447→{mcd_bracket}
   448→and remains constant throughout.
   449→{smc_cohort_fief_s}
   450→cannot contain
   451→{smc_timer_s},
   452→simplifying parallel execution semantics.
   453→
   454→=== Queue Architecture and Executive Cycle
   455→The
   456→{pmp_executive_cycle}
   457→enforces deterministic event processing within each
   458→{pmp_realm}
   459→through a strict three-phase ordering:
   460→
   461→* *Timer Expiration Phase*: All
   462→{smc_timer_s}
   463→scheduled to expire at or before the current time trigger their corresponding
   464→{hdm_timeout}
   465→handlers
   466→* *Ledger Drainage*: All
   467→{mcd_message_s}
   468→placed on the
   469→{pmp_ledger}
   470→by handlers during the current cycle are delivered to their target
   471→{smc_ward_s}
   472→within the same
   473→{pmp_realm},
   474→skipping those marked as elided
   475→* *Ingress Queue Processing*: External
   476→{mcd_message_s}
   477→from other
   478→{pmp_realm_s}
   479→are processed from the
   480→{pmp_ingress_queue}.
   481→
   482→The
   483→{pmp_ledger}
   484→must be completely drained before proceeding to the next phase, preventing event interleaving and ensuring causal consistency. Messages generated by
   485→{hdm_receiver_s}
   486→and
   487→{hdm_timeout_s}
   488→during a single cycle remain ordered within that cycle.
   489→The
   490→{pmp_ledger}
   491→enables
   492→{pmp_elision}
   493→optimization where same-{pmp_realm}
   494→{gcm_sender}
   495→invocations bypass serialization by directly calling target
   496→{hdm_receiver_s},
   497→provided no handler currently executes on that
   498→{smc_ward}.
   499→Elided messages are marked in the
   500→{pmp_ledger}
   501→to skip processing during drainage while preserving trace data.
   502→{pmp_watch}
   503→rules capture specified
   504→{mcd_field_s}
   505→even from elided calls, ensuring critical state transitions remain visible.
   506→The
   507→{pmp_ingress_queue}
   508→processes messages only after all
   509→{smc_timer}
   510→expirations and
   511→{pmp_ledger}
   512→drainage, establishing a priority hierarchy where local reactions complete before processing external stimuli.
   513→The
   514→{smc_topology}
   515→pattern determines queue structures within each
   516→{pmp_realm}:
   517→{smc_topology_monolithic}:
   518→{pmp_ingress_queue}
   519→degenerates to a pending event buffer
   520→{smc_topology_paired}:
   521→Single
   522→{pmp_ingress_queue}
   523→receives from peer
   524→{pmp_realm}
   525→{smc_topology_switchboard}:
   526→Both
   527→{pmp_ingress_queue}
   528→(from
   529→{pmp_switchboard})
   530→and
   531→{pmp_egress_queue}
   532→(to
   533→{pmp_switchboard}).
   534→
   535→{smc_cohort_fief_s}
   536→introduce a queue hierarchy where the
   537→{smc_fief}-ledger
   538→drains completely before the
   539→{pmp_realm}
   540→processes its ledger.
   541→This maintains causality within parallel processing domains.
   542→
   543→At cycle completion, the entire
   544→{pmp_ledger}
   545→transfers to the
   546→{pmp_egress_queue},
   547→providing complete execution history for analysis including both queued and elided message flows.
   548→
   549→=== Time Source Independence
   550→
   551→The
   552→{smc_forge}
   553→selects a
   554→{smc_time_source}
   555→through the
   556→{smc_fixture}
   557→chosen, independently from execution model and topology:
   558→
   559→* Virtual
   560→{smc_time_source}
   561→+ VirtualMonolithicFixture: Deterministic single-threaded testing
   562→* Real
   563→{smc_time_source}
   564→+ VirtualMonolithicFixture: Race condition discovery under controlled observation
   565→* Virtual
   566→{smc_time_source}
   567→+ VirtualDistributedFixture: Reproducible concurrent load testing
   568→* Real
   569→{smc_time_source}
   570→+ ProductionFixture: Production deployment with wall-clock fidelity
   571→
   572→The
   573→{toc_advance} {toc_directive}
   574→provides unified temporal control across all combinations, advancing virtual time explicitly or waiting for real time to elapse.
   575→
   576→=== Transport Performance Characteristics
   577→
   578→{smc_transport}
   579→mechanisms offer different tradeoffs within topology constraints:
   580→
   581→* {smc_transport_inprocess}:
   582→Zero-copy, nanosecond latency, no isolation
   583→* {smc_transport_sharedmemory}:
   584→Near-zero-copy, microsecond latency, process isolation
   585→* {smc_transport_localhostudp}:
   586→Kernel-managed queues, millisecond latency, debugging visibility
   587→* {smc_transport_networkudp}:
   588→Geographic distribution, variable latency, internet scale
   589→
   590→The
   591→{smc_shape}
   592→may mix
   593→{smc_transport}
   594→mechanisms within a
   595→{smc_alliance},
   596→optimizing critical paths while maintaining operational simplicity elsewhere.
   597→
   598→=== Binary Protocol Addressing
   599→
   600→The
   601→{pmp_binary_format}
   602→employs variable addressing depth based on message scope:
   603→
   604→* {pmp_elision}-optimized local calls: {smc_ward_id} only
   605→* Same-{pmp_realm} queued: {smc_ward} type + {smc_ward_id}
   606→* Cross-{pmp_realm}: {smc_realm_id} + {smc_ward} type + {smc_ward_id}
   607→* Future cross-server: Server ID + {smc_realm_id} + {smc_ward} type + {smc_ward_id}
   608→
   609→Message headers adapt to the minimum addressing required for unambiguous routing, reducing overhead for local communications while maintaining full addressing for distributed scenarios.
   610→
   611→== Forge Patterns
   612→
   613→The
   614→{smc_forge}
   615→enables comprehensive testing by running test suites against multiple
   616→{smc_fixture_s}
   617→generated from a
   618→{smc_shape}.
   619→These patterns provide mechanisms for test isolation and control.
   620→The code generator automatically detects structural gaps between
   621→{smc_accord}
   622→definitions and
   623→{smc_shape}
   624→selections, generating appropriate test interfaces when management relationships exist in the
   625→{smc_accord}
   626→but corresponding
   627→{smc_ward_s}
   628→are absent from the
   629→{smc_shape}.
   630→This detection-based generation ensures
   631→{smc_forge_case_s}
   632→maintain full lifecycle and communication control even when testing partial system configurations.
   633→
   634→=== Test Control Interfaces
   635→
   636→The code generator analyzes structural gaps between
   637→{smc_accord}
   638→definitions and
   639→{smc_shape}
   640→selections to determine which test interfaces to generate.
   641→When a
   642→{smc_shape}
   643→includes
   644→{smc_fief_s}
   645→without their managing
   646→{smc_gerent_s}
   647→(as defined in the
   648→{smc_accord}),
   649→the generator creates
   650→{toc_regent_ward}
   651→interfaces.
   652→When
   653→{smc_tenant} {smc_ward_s}
   654→lack their bound
   655→{smc_steward_s},
   656→the generator creates
   657→{toc_bailiff_ward}
   658→interfaces.
   659→Both patterns ensure
   660→{smc_forge_case_s}
   661→maintain full lifecycle and communication control even when testing partial system configurations.
   662→
   663→{toc_regent_ward_s}
   664→exercise the full management authority of absent
   665→{smc_gerent_s},
   666→including target
   667→{pmp_realm}
   668→selection for new
   669→{smc_fief}
   670→instances,
   671→{smc_steward}
   672→binding decisions respecting
   673→{smc_locality_s},
   674→and management message transmission to
   675→{smc_vassal_s}.
   676→The
   677→{toc_regent_levy}
   678→operation completes synchronously, with the
   679→{smc_fixture}
   680→emitting
   681→{noc_regent_allocation}
   682→upon successful creation.
   683→This notice provides complete addressing information needed for subsequent test operations.
   684→{toc_regent_dissolve}
   685→triggers proper cleanup sequences including
   686→{hdm_vacate}
   687→notifications to bound
   688→{smc_steward_s}.
   689→
   690→{toc_bailiff_ward_s}
   691→maintain full
   692→{smc_shadow}
   693→state for all
   694→{smc_bond_s}
   695→the absent
   696→{smc_steward}
   697→would foster.
   698→The message flow operates through three stages:
   699→{toc_bailiff_bind}
   700→creates necessary
   701→{smc_shadow}
   702→state and routing entries,
   703→{toc_bailiff_send}
   704→respects
   705→{mcd_message}
   706→types defined in
   707→{mcd_bracket_s}
   708→between the
   709→{smc_steward}
   710→and
   711→{smc_tenant},
   712→and when
   713→{smc_tenant_s}
   714→send messages to their absent
   715→{smc_steward},
   716→{noc_bailiff_receive}
   717→provides both the
   718→{toc_missive}
   719→representation and read-only
   720→{smc_shadow}
   721→access.
   722→When a
   723→{smc_fief}
   724→containing
   725→{smc_tenant_s}
   726→is created by external means,
   727→{noc_bailiff_lodge}
   728→alerts the
   729→{smc_forge_case}
   730→to establish appropriate connections.
   731→
   732→The code generator creates
   733→{toc_missive}
   734→types from
   735→{smc_accord} {mcd_message}
   736→definitions, providing type-safe test operations with native language types, optional metadata including timestamps, routing information, and correlation identifiers, and separation from
   737→{pmp_binary_format}
   738→machinery.
   739→{toc_missive_s}
   740→appear in
   741→{toc_regent_send}
   742→and
   743→{toc_bailiff_send}
   744→directives and within
   745→{noc_bailiff_receive}
   746→notices.
   747→
   748→=== Test Execution Control
   749→
   750→{toc_intervention_s}
   751→execute synchronously within
   752→{noc_notice}
   753→callbacks, ensuring deterministic execution before the
   754→{pmp_executive_cycle}
   755→resumes, atomic completion of all state modifications, and system consistency maintenance across interventions.
   756→Available interventions include
   757→{toc_supplant}
   758→for message routing control and state manipulation operations for
   759→{toc_synthetic_fief_s}.
   760→All interventions complete atomically before the
   761→{pmp_executive_cycle}
   762→resumes, maintaining system consistency.
   763→
   764→Each
   765→{toc_directive}
   766→represents a complete, atomic operation that may trigger multiple
   767→{pmp_executive_cycle_s}
   768→to reach completion.
   769→{toc_advance}
   770→processes all timer expirations and cascading effects within the specified duration.
   771→{toc_next_timeout}
   772→executes the complete cycle for the next pending timer, jumping directly to that moment.
   773→{toc_query}
   774→operates only between cycles when the system is quiescent, returning immutable snapshots.
   775→{toc_inject}
   776→enables simulation of external interactions and edge cases by introducing
   777→{mcd_message_s}
   778→that would not arise from normal
   779→{smc_ward}
   780→interactions.
   781→The bidirectional flow between
   782→{toc_directive_s}
   783→controlling execution and
   784→{noc_notice_s}
   785→observing behavior creates deterministic test execution with full visibility.
   786→
   787→{noc_notice_s}
   788→provide visibility at specific points in the
   789→{pmp_executive_cycle}:
   790→{noc_message_queued}
   791→when a handler places a message on the
   792→{pmp_ledger},
   793→{noc_message_dispatched}
   794→when
   795→{gcm_dispatch}
   796→delivers to the target
   797→{smc_ward},
   798→{noc_message_emitted}
   799→when a message targets a different
   800→{pmp_realm},
   801→and
   802→{noc_timer_expired}
   803→when a timer triggers its
   804→{hdm_timeout}
   805→handler.
   806→Each notice includes complete addressing information through the tuple of
   807→{smc_realm_id}, {smc_fief_id}, {smc_ward_id}
   808→and relevant payload data.
   809→
   810→The
   811→{smc_fixture}
   812→controls execution through this precise interleaving of
   813→{toc_directive_s}
   814→and
   815→{noc_notice_s}.
   816→Test code issues directives to advance time, inject messages, or query state.
   817→The realm processes these directives, potentially triggering multiple
   818→{pmp_executive_cycle_s}.
   819→During processing, the realm emits notices for significant events.
   820→Test code receives notices and may invoke
   821→{toc_intervention_s}
   822→within notice handlers.
   823→This pattern enables both passive observation and active manipulation of system behavior.
   824→
   825→=== Test Replacement Strategies
   826→
   827→{toc_synthetic_fief_s}
   828→enable complete behavioral replacement while maintaining structural compatibility.
   829→Test code provides mock implementations of every
   830→{hdm_receiver} and {hdm_timeout},
   831→full access to
   832→{smc_property}
   833→definitions for state verification, and simulation of external systems, failure modes, and edge cases impossible to reproduce with real implementations.
   834→This pattern supports isolated testing of dependent
   835→{smc_fief_s}
   836→without requiring their actual dependencies.
   837→The
   838→{smc_forge}
   839→generates infrastructure allowing test code to provide complete mock implementations while accessing all
   840→{smc_property}
   841→definitions from the original
   842→{smc_fief}
   843→specification.
   844→
   845→For
   846→{toc_interceptable_ward}
   847→types, the message flow follows a precise pattern where all
   848→{mcd_message_s}
   849→and
   850→{smc_timer}
   851→expirations route to the
   852→{toc_ward_interceptor}
   853→before potentially reaching original
   854→{gcm_handler_s}.
   855→The interceptor has full access to each instance's
   856→{smc_property_s}
   857→for inspection and modification.
   858→{toc_supplant}
   859→controls per-instance routing: when invoked with true, messages bypass original
   860→{gcm_handler_s}
   861→entirely; when false, messages forward to original handlers after interception.
   862→Routing configuration persists for each instance until explicitly changed, enabling surgical test control without full replacement.
   863→Each interceptor receives all events for all instances of the
   864→{toc_interceptable_ward}
   865→type, allowing coordinated control across multiple instances.
   866→
   867→The test mechanisms compose to support multiple isolation strategies.
   868→Partial system testing uses
   869→{toc_regent_ward_s} and {toc_bailiff_ward_s}
   870→to test subsets of the system with full control over missing components.
   871→Behavioral replacement deploys
   872→{toc_synthetic_fief_s}
   873→for external dependencies, enabling simulation of failure modes and edge cases.
   874→Surgical control applies
   875→{toc_ward_interceptor_s}
   876→to specific interaction points without disrupting the rest of the system.
   877→Time control leverages virtual
   878→{smc_time_source}
   879→with explicit advancement through
   880→{toc_advance}
   881→and
   882→{toc_next_timeout}
   883→directives.
   884→These strategies compose freely - a single test might use regent cells to manage lifecycle, bailiff cells to simulate external services, interceptors to observe specific interactions, and virtual time to ensure deterministic execution.
   885→
   886→== Definitions
   887→
   888→[[smc_wardrealmsubstrate]]
   889→{smc_wardrealmsubstrate}::
   890→A substrate for constructing distributed state machine systems through disciplined message exchange between participants.
   891→Provides foundational abstractions of
   892→{smc_ward_s},
   893→{mcd_message_s},
   894→{smc_fief_s},
   895→and
   896→{pmp_realm_s},
   897→along with code generation infrastructure that produces
   898→{pmp_transport_context}
   899→machinery from
   900→{smc_accord}
   901→specifications.
   902→
   903→[[smc_ward]]
   904→{smc_ward}::
   905→A
   906→{mcd_message}
   907→and
   908→{smc_timer}
   909→driven state machine with a defined
   910→{mcd_message_ing}
   911→interface that maintains local state through
   912→{smc_property_s},
   913→manages
   914→{smc_bond_s}
   915→to other
   916→{smc_ward_s},
   917→and executes within a
   918→{pmp_realm}.
   919→Each
   920→{smc_ward}
   921→sends and receives specific
   922→{mcd_message_s}
   923→as defined by its assigned
   924→{mcd_bracket_s}
   925→within its
   926→{smc_bond_s}.
   927→Each
   928→{smc_ward}
   929→may designate one enumeration
   930→{smc_property}
   931→as its
   932→{smc_posture}.
   933→
   934→[[smc_fief]]
   935→{smc_fief}::
   936→A type specification defining a set of
   937→{smc_vassal} {smc_ward}
   938→types that share a lifecycle, along with any required
   939→{smc_steward} {smc_ward}
   940→bindings.
   941→Each
   942→{smc_fief}
   943→type may specify co-location constraints and
   944→{pmp_backend}
   945→requirements that influence placement decisions during founding.
   946→When declaring
   947→{smc_steward}
   948→relationships, each binding may specify a
   949→{smc_locality}
   950→that restricts where the
   951→{smc_vassal_s}
   952→can be placed relative to their bound
   953→{smc_steward}.
   954→At runtime,
   955→{smc_fief}
   956→instances are created within
   957→{pmp_realm_s}
   958→by
   959→{smc_gerent_s}
   960→through
   961→{gcm_levy}
   962→invocations, respecting both
   963→{smc_shape}
   964→topology constraints and co-location requirements.
   965→Each instance contains exactly one running
   966→{smc_ward}
   967→of each type defined in the
   968→{smc_fief}
   969→specification, bound to specific
   970→{smc_steward}
   971→instances selected by the
   972→{smc_gerent}
   973→at allocation time.
   974→The
   975→{smc_gerent}
   976→destroys
   977→{smc_fief}
   978→instances via
   979→{gcm_dissolver}
   980→invocations.
   981→Each
   982→{smc_ward}
   983→type belongs to exactly one
   984→{smc_fief}
   985→type, and each
   986→{smc_gerent}
   987→must reside in a different
   988→{smc_fief}
   989→type than those it manages, preventing circular dependencies.
   990→{smc_fief_s}
   991→are designated as
   992→{smc_hearth_s}
   993→by specifying no
   994→{smc_gerent}
   995→during definition, indicating automatic instantiation when their containing
   996→{pmp_realm}
   997→starts.
   998→
   999→[[smc_cohort_fief]]
  1000→{smc_cohort_fief}::
  1001→A
  1002→{smc_fief}
  1003→type with an internal
  1004→{pmp_ledger}
  1005→enabling parallel
  1006→{smc_ward}
  1007→instance execution.
  1008→Queue hierarchy and execution semantics are detailed in
  1009→{smc_architecture_patterns}.
  1010→
  1011→[[smc_vassal]]
  1012→{smc_vassal}::
  1013→A
  1014→{smc_ward}
  1015→type that exists only within a
  1016→{smc_fief}
  1017→instance, restricted to communicating with
  1018→{smc_ward_s}
  1019→whose instances were identified at
  1020→{smc_fief}
  1021→allocation time.
  1022→The
  1023→{smc_accord}
  1024→dictates whether a given
  1025→{smc_vassal}
  1026→acts as a
  1027→{smc_tenant}
  1028→for external
  1029→{smc_steward_s}.
  1030→
  1031→[[smc_steward]]
  1032→{smc_steward}::
  1033→A
  1034→{smc_ward}
  1035→that participates in cross-{smc_fief}
  1036→messaging relationships with
  1037→{smc_tenant} {smc_ward_s}
  1038→in other
  1039→{smc_fief_s}.
  1040→{smc_steward_s}
  1041→are bound to
  1042→{smc_tenant_s}
  1043→at
  1044→{smc_fief}
  1045→allocation time through the
  1046→{gcm_levy}
  1047→mechanism, with the specific
  1048→{smc_steward}
  1049→instance selected by the
  1050→{smc_gerent}
  1051→during allocation.
  1052→The
  1053→{smc_fief}
  1054→specification may impose
  1055→{smc_locality_s}
  1056→on each
  1057→{smc_steward}
  1058→binding, requiring the
  1059→{smc_gerent}
  1060→to honor co-location requirements when selecting both the target
  1061→{pmp_realm}
  1062→and specific
  1063→{smc_steward}
  1064→instance.
  1065→When a
  1066→{smc_tenant_p}
  1067→containing
  1068→{smc_fief}
  1069→is created, the
  1070→{smc_steward}
  1071→receives an
  1072→{hdm_lodge}
  1073→notification; when destroyed, it receives an
  1074→{hdm_vacate}
  1075→notification.
  1076→{smc_steward_s}
  1077→and
  1078→{smc_tenant_s}
  1079→exchange
  1080→{mcd_message_s}
  1081→using the same queue mechanisms as any other
  1082→{smc_ward_s}
  1083→(internal when co-located in the same
  1084→{pmp_realm},
  1085→external otherwise), with the specific
  1086→{mcd_message_s}
  1087→and interaction patterns defined through
  1088→{mcd_bracket}
  1089→participation or other messaging mechanisms specified in the
  1090→{smc_accord}.
  1091→Each
  1092→{smc_steward}
  1093→may foster a
  1094→{smc_bond}
  1095→type specific to each
  1096→{smc_tenant}
  1097→type it serves, creating per-connection
  1098→{smc_shadow_s}
  1099→for relationship-specific state.
  1100→[[smc_tenant]]
  1101→{smc_tenant}::
  1102→A
  1103→{smc_vassal} {smc_ward}
  1104→that exchanges
  1105→{mcd_message_s}
  1106→with a
  1107→{smc_steward}
  1108→in a different
  1109→{smc_fief}.
  1110→The
  1111→{smc_tenant}-to-{smc_steward}
  1112→binding is established when the
  1113→{smc_tenant_p}
  1114→containing
  1115→{smc_fief}
  1116→is allocated, with the specific
  1117→{smc_steward}
  1118→instance selected by the
  1119→{smc_gerent}
  1120→during
  1121→{gcm_levy}.
  1122→{smc_tenant_s}
  1123→and
  1124→{smc_steward_s}
  1125→communicate through
  1126→{mcd_message_s}
  1127→defined by the
  1128→{smc_accord},
  1129→following normal message routing rules where messages traverse the
  1130→{pmp_ledger}
  1131→when both
  1132→{smc_ward_s}
  1133→reside in the same
  1134→{pmp_realm}
  1135→or the
  1136→{pmp_ingress_queue}
  1137→when they reside in different
  1138→{pmp_realm_s}.
  1139→
  1140→[[smc_bond]]
  1141→{smc_bond}::
  1142→A specification template defining reusable per-connection state and
  1143→{mcd_message_ing}
  1144→capabilities between
  1145→{smc_ward_s}.
  1146→Each
  1147→{smc_bond}
  1148→accumulates participation in multiple
  1149→{mcd_bracket_s},
  1150→declaring for each whether the fostering
  1151→{smc_ward}
  1152→acts as
  1153→{mcd_proactor}
  1154→or
  1155→{mcd_reactor}.
  1156→At runtime, each established connection instantiates a
  1157→{smc_shadow}
  1158→containing the
  1159→{smc_property_s},
  1160→{smc_timer_s},
  1161→and
  1162→{smc_retinue_s}
  1163→defined by the
  1164→{smc_bond}.
  1165→A
  1166→{smc_ward}
  1167→may foster at most one
  1168→{smc_bond}
  1169→type to any given remote
  1170→{smc_ward}
  1171→type.
  1172→
  1173→[[mcd_message]]
  1174→{mcd_message}::
  1175→A structured data unit consisting of typed
  1176→{mcd_field_s}
  1177→that follows the
  1178→{pmp_binary_format}
  1179→for transmission through the system.
  1180→Messages are defined in one of two ways:
  1181→within a
  1182→{mcd_bracket}
  1183→to implement a specific
  1184→{mcd_phase}
  1185→of an
  1186→{mcd_exchange},
  1187→with direction determined by the
  1188→{mcd_phase}
  1189→specification and the
  1190→{smc_ward}
  1191→roles of
  1192→{mcd_proactor}
  1193→and
  1194→{mcd_reactor};
  1195→or at the
  1196→{smc_fief}
  1197→level for direct communication between
  1198→{smc_ward_s}
  1199→within that
  1200→{smc_fief}.
  1201→
  1202→[[mcd_field]]
  1203→{mcd_field}::
  1204→A named, typed data element within a
  1205→{mcd_message}.
  1206→Each
  1207→{mcd_field}
  1208→has a specific
  1209→{ftc_form}
  1210→and follows the
  1211→{pmp_binary_format}
  1212→serialization rules defined by that
  1213→{ftc_form}.
  1214→
  1215→[[mcd_summons]]
  1216→{mcd_summons}::
  1217→A
  1218→{mcd_phase}
  1219→that sends to all
  1220→{smc_retinue}
  1221→members simultaneously.
  1222→
  1223→[[ftc_bound]]
  1224→{ftc_bound}::
  1225→A constraint that references a
  1226→{smc_constant}
  1227→to define array sizing within
  1228→{mcd_message_s} and {smc_property_s}.
  1229→Each
  1230→{ftc_bound}
  1231→generates a corresponding
  1232→{ftc_index}
  1233→type for type-safe
  1234→{ftc_array}
  1235→access.
  1236→
  1237→[[smc_accord]]
  1238→{smc_accord}::
  1239→A specification that defines the complete
  1240→{mcd_message_ing}
  1241→interface between
  1242→{smc_ward_s}
  1243→in a system as well as the full set of
  1244→{smc_shape}
  1245→definitions for complete code generation specification.
  1246→The
  1247→{smc_accord}
  1248→declaratively describes all
  1249→{mcd_message_s},
  1250→their
  1251→{mcd_field_s},
  1252→the relationships between communicating
  1253→{smc_ward_s},
  1254→and the
  1255→{smc_fief}
  1256→organizational structure.
  1257→
  1258→[[smc_timer]]
  1259→{smc_timer}::
  1260→A time-based event mechanism that exists as both a specification-time declaration and a runtime schedulable entity.
  1261→In
  1262→{pmp_specification_context}, {smc_timer_s}
  1263→are declared for
  1264→{smc_ward_s}
  1265→directly or within
  1266→{smc_bond_s}
  1267→for per-connection timing needs.
  1268→At runtime in
  1269→{pmp_business_context},
  1270→each declared
  1271→{smc_timer}
  1272→can be armed to trigger after a specified duration or cleared before expiration.
  1273→When a
  1274→{smc_timer}
  1275→expires,
  1276→{pmp_transport_context}
  1277→generates a
  1278→{hdm_timeout}
  1279→event that invokes the corresponding handler in
  1280→{pmp_business_context}.
  1281→Timer precision varies by deployment context, from microsecond-level precision in interrupt-hosted
  1282→{pmp_realm_s}
  1283→to millisecond precision in standard operating system threads.
  1284→
  1285→[[smc_retinue]]
  1286→{smc_retinue}::
  1287→A multicast grouping mechanism that exists as both a specification-time declaration and a runtime membership registry.
  1288→In
  1289→{pmp_specification_context}, {smc_retinue_s}
  1290→are declared for
  1291→{smc_steward_s}
  1292→or within
  1293→{smc_bond_s}
  1294→for per-connection groups.
  1295→At runtime in
  1296→{pmp_transport_context},
  1297→each declared
  1298→{smc_retinue}
  1299→becomes an active membership registry that tracks which
  1300→{smc_tenant_s}
  1301→belong to the named group, with membership managed through
  1302→{gcm_enlist} and {gcm_discharge}
  1303→operations.
  1304→When a
  1305→{mcd_phase}
  1306→is marked for multicast in a
  1307→{mcd_bracket},
  1308→the corresponding
  1309→{gcm_sender}
  1310→requires a
  1311→{smc_retinue}
  1312→identifier to select recipients from the runtime membership.
  1313→Once selected by the first
  1314→{gcm_sender}
  1315→invocation within a
  1316→{mcd_bracket},
  1317→{smc_retinue} membership remains locked for the rest of the
  1318→{mcd_bracket}.
  1319→
  1320→[[smc_harvest]]
  1321→{smc_harvest}::
  1322→Parallel-safe per-connection storage created during
  1323→{mcd_summons}
  1324→operations for gathering results from multiple
  1325→{smc_tenant_s}.
  1326→Each
  1327→{gcm_glean}
  1328→handler writes to a separate
  1329→{smc_harvest}
  1330→instance without synchronization, enabling safe parallel execution.
  1331→After all
  1332→{gcm_glean}
  1333→handlers complete, the
  1334→{gcm_thresh}
  1335→handler accesses the gathered
  1336→{smc_harvest}
  1337→data to produce consolidated results.
  1338→Unlike
  1339→{smc_shadow_s}
  1340→which persist for the connection lifetime,
  1341→{smc_harvest_s}
  1342→exist only for the duration of the
  1343→{mcd_summons}
  1344→processing.
  1345→
  1346→[[mcd_exchange]]
  1347→{mcd_exchange}::
  1348→An abstract messaging protocol specification that defines named
  1349→{mcd_phase_s}
  1350→without implementation details.
  1351→Each
  1352→{mcd_phase}
  1353→declares a logical interaction point and its directionality within the protocol.
  1354→{mcd_exchange_s}
  1355→enable reuse of interaction patterns across different
  1356→{mcd_bracket}
  1357→implementations.
  1358→Multiple
  1359→{mcd_bracket_s}
  1360→may implement the same
  1361→{mcd_exchange},
  1362→each providing different
  1363→{mcd_message}
  1364→structures for the same abstract
  1365→{mcd_phase_s}.
  1366→
  1367→[[mcd_bracket]]
  1368→{mcd_bracket}::
  1369→A concrete messaging interface that implements an
  1370→{mcd_exchange}
  1371→by binding abstract
  1372→{mcd_phase_s}
  1373→to actual
  1374→{mcd_message}
  1375→definitions.
  1376→Each
  1377→{mcd_bracket}
  1378→defines two complementary roles:
  1379→{mcd_proactor}
  1380→(the initiating participant) and
  1381→{mcd_reactor}
  1382→(the responding participant).
  1383→{smc_ward_s}
  1384→declare participation in a
  1385→{mcd_bracket}
  1386→by assuming one of these roles, either directly or through a
  1387→{smc_bond}.
  1388→The role assignment determines which
  1389→{mcd_message_s}
  1390→a
  1391→{smc_ward}
  1392→may send or receive within that
  1393→{mcd_bracket_p}
  1394→context.
  1395→
  1396→[[mcd_phase]]
  1397→{mcd_phase}::
  1398→A named interaction point within an
  1399→{mcd_exchange}
  1400→that represents an abstract message flow without specifying concrete data structure.
  1401→Each
  1402→{mcd_phase}
  1403→declares its directionality: messages implementing the
  1404→{mcd_phase}
  1405→flow either from
  1406→{mcd_proactor}
  1407→to
  1408→{mcd_reactor}
  1409→or from
  1410→{mcd_reactor}
  1411→to
  1412→{mcd_proactor}.
  1413→{mcd_phase_s}
  1414→enable protocol abstraction, allowing multiple
  1415→{mcd_bracket_s}
  1416→to provide different concrete implementations of the same logical interaction pattern.
  1417→{mcd_phase_s}
  1418→may be marked for
  1419→{mcd_summons},
  1420→enabling parallel processing for
  1421→{smc_cohort_fief_s}.
  1422→
  1423→[[mcd_proactor]]
  1424→{mcd_proactor}::
  1425→The initiating role within a
  1426→{mcd_bracket_p}
  1427→messaging pattern, typically representing the party that begins an interaction or controls the protocol flow.
  1428→A
  1429→{smc_ward}
  1430→assumes the
  1431→{mcd_proactor}
  1432→role for a
  1433→{mcd_bracket}
  1434→either directly or when fostering a
  1435→{smc_bond}
  1436→that declares
  1437→{mcd_proactor}
  1438→participation for that
  1439→{mcd_bracket}.
  1440→The
  1441→{mcd_proactor}
  1442→sends
  1443→{mcd_message_s}
  1444→for
  1445→{mcd_phase_s}
  1446→directed to the
  1447→{mcd_reactor}
  1448→and receives
  1449→{mcd_message_s}
  1450→for
  1451→{mcd_phase_s}
  1452→directed from the
  1453→{mcd_reactor}.
  1454→
  1455→[[mcd_reactor]]
  1456→{mcd_reactor}::
  1457→The responding role within a
  1458→{mcd_bracket_p}
  1459→messaging pattern, typically representing the party that reacts to or services requests from the
  1460→{mcd_proactor}.
  1461→A
  1462→{smc_ward}
  1463→assumes the
  1464→{mcd_reactor}
  1465→role for a
  1466→{mcd_bracket}
  1467→either directly or when fostering a
  1468→{smc_bond}
  1469→that declares
  1470→{mcd_reactor}
  1471→participation for that
  1472→{mcd_bracket}.
  1473→The
  1474→{mcd_reactor}
  1475→receives
  1476→{mcd_message_s}
  1477→for
  1478→{mcd_phase_s}
  1479→directed to it and sends
  1480→{mcd_message_s}
  1481→for
  1482→{mcd_phase_s}
  1483→directed to the
  1484→{mcd_proactor}.
  1485→
  1486→[[pmp_realm]]
  1487→{pmp_realm}::
  1488→A single-threaded runtime container that hosts multiple instances of different
  1489→{smc_fief}
  1490→types and enforces synchronous
  1491→{mcd_message}
  1492→processing within its boundaries.
  1493→Implements the
  1494→{pmp_executive_cycle}
  1495→and message routing as described in
  1496→{smc_architecture_patterns}.
  1497→
  1498→[[pmp_executive_cycle]]
  1499→{pmp_executive_cycle}::
  1500→The deterministic event processing loop within a
  1501→{pmp_realm}
  1502→that ensures causally consistent message and timer handling through strict phase ordering.
  1503→See
  1504→{smc_architecture_patterns}
  1505→for the execution mechanics and interaction with queue structures.
  1506→
  1507→[[pmp_binary_format]]
  1508→{pmp_binary_format}::
  1509→The singular, standardized serialization format used for all
  1510→{mcd_message_s}
  1511→exchanged between
  1512→{smc_ward_s},
  1513→regardless of their
  1514→{pmp_backend}.
  1515→The
  1516→{pmp_binary_format}
  1517→ensures interoperability across language boundaries and provides efficient cache handling.
  1518→
  1519→[[pmp_specification_context]]
  1520→{pmp_specification_context}::
  1521→The domain of
  1522→{smc_accord}
  1523→processing where type relationships are defined, validated, and transformed into
  1524→{pmp_code_generator}
  1525→inputs.
  1526→{pmp_specification_context}
  1527→validates that all
  1528→{smc_bond}
  1529→requirements are satisfied before permitting code generation.
  1530→{pmp_specification_context}
  1531→exists only during
  1532→{smc_accord}
  1533→compilation and establishes the complete type system and relationship templates that will govern runtime behavior.
  1534→Unlike
  1535→{pmp_business_context} and {pmp_transport_context}
  1536→which exist at runtime,
  1537→{pmp_specification_context}
  1538→artifacts exist only as templates that shape generated code.
  1539→
  1540→[[pmp_business_context]]
  1541→{pmp_business_context}::
  1542→The domain of developer-written code that implements business logic within
  1543→{gcm_handler_s}.
  1544→{pmp_business_context}
  1545→code operates on
  1546→{smc_ward}
  1547→state and determines which, if any,
  1548→{gcm_utility_s}
  1549→to invoke in response to incoming messages.
  1550→
  1551→[[pmp_transport_context]]
  1552→{pmp_transport_context}::
  1553→Generated code that handles message serialization, routing, and protocol machinery.
  1554→{pmp_transport_context}
  1555→code is produced by the
  1556→{pmp_code_generator}
  1557→and provides the infrastructure connecting
  1558→{pmp_business_context}
  1559→components.
  1560→
  1561→[[pmp_backend]]
  1562→{pmp_backend}::
  1563→A target programming language/ environment implementation for the
  1564→{mcd_message_ing}
  1565→system.
  1566→Each
  1567→{pmp_backend}
  1568→produces language-specific code from the
  1569→{smc_accord}
  1570→while maintaining compatibility with the
  1571→{pmp_binary_format},
  1572→allowing cross-platform communication.
  1573→
  1574→[[pmp_code_generator]]
  1575→{pmp_code_generator}::
  1576→The process that transforms an
  1577→{smc_accord}
  1578→into executable code for a specific
  1579→{pmp_backend}.
  1580→The
  1581→{pmp_code_generator}
  1582→produces both
  1583→{pmp_transport_context}
  1584→components and interfaces for
  1585→{pmp_business_context}
  1586→implementation.
  1587→
  1588→[[gcm_dispatch]]
  1589→{gcm_dispatch}::
  1590→The generated mechanism within a
  1591→{pmp_realm}
  1592→that receives binary message data, determines the target
  1593→{smc_ward}
  1594→and message type, deserializes the data, and invokes the appropriate
  1595→{hdm_receiver}.
  1596→{gcm_dispatch} routes messages to the appropriate
  1597→{smc_ward}
  1598→instance within the
  1599→{pmp_realm_p}
  1600→single-threaded execution context.
  1601→
  1602→[[gcm_renderer]]
  1603→{gcm_renderer}::
  1604→A generated function that creates human-readable representations of
  1605→{mcd_message_s}.
  1606→{gcm_renderer_s}
  1607→are
  1608→{pmp_backend}-specific
  1609→and support debugging and monitoring of system activity.
  1610→
  1611→[[gcm_enlist]]
  1612→{gcm_enlist}::
  1613→A
  1614→{gcm_utility}
  1615→that
  1616→{smc_steward_s}
  1617→invoke to add a specific
  1618→{smc_tenant}
  1619→to a named
  1620→{smc_retinue}.
  1621→{gcm_enlist}
  1622→operates entirely within
  1623→{pmp_transport_context}
  1624→to maintain efficient multicast group membership.
  1625→
  1626→[[gcm_discharge]]
  1627→{gcm_discharge}::
  1628→A
  1629→{gcm_utility}
  1630→that
  1631→{smc_steward_s}
  1632→invoke to remove a specific
  1633→{smc_tenant}
  1634→from a named
  1635→{smc_retinue}.
  1636→{gcm_discharge}
  1637→operates entirely within
  1638→{pmp_transport_context}
  1639→to maintain efficient multicast group membership.
  1640→
  1641→[[gcm_glean]]
  1642→{gcm_glean}::
  1643→A
  1644→{gcm_handler}
  1645→triggered by the response to a
  1646→{mcd_summons} {mcd_phase}
  1647→restricted to reading
  1648→{smc_ward}
  1649→properties and writing
  1650→{smc_harvest}
  1651→entries, enabling parallel processing.
  1652→Cannot fail or timeout.
  1653→
  1654→[[gcm_thresh]]
  1655→{gcm_thresh}::
  1656→A
  1657→{gcm_handler}
  1658→invoked after all
  1659→{gcm_glean} {gcm_handler_s}
  1660→complete, providing access to gathered
  1661→{smc_harvest}
  1662→data from a
  1663→{mcd_summons}.
  1664→
  1665→[[ftc_form]]
  1666→{ftc_form}::
  1667→A type category encompassing all data types usable in
  1668→{smc_property_s},
  1669→{mcd_field_s},
  1670→and
  1671→{smc_shadow_s}.
  1672→{ftc_form_s}
  1673→include
  1674→{ftc_primitive_s},
  1675→{ftc_domain_type_s},
  1676→{ftc_enumeration_s},
  1677→{ftc_record_s},
  1678→and arrays characterized by
  1679→{ftc_bound_s}.
  1680→Each
  1681→{ftc_form}
  1682→defines serialization rules for the
  1683→{pmp_binary_format}
  1684→and memory layout constraints.
  1685→
  1686→[[ftc_primitive]]
  1687→{ftc_primitive}::
  1688→A
  1689→{ftc_form}
  1690→representing processor-native formats: signed integers (I8, I16, I32, I64), unsigned integers (U8, U16, U32, U64), floating point (f32, f64), boolean, and character.
  1691→{ftc_primitive_s}
  1692→provide the atomic building blocks for all other
  1693→{ftc_form_s}
  1694→and map directly to hardware representations for efficient processing.
  1695→
  1696→[[ftc_domain_type]]
  1697→{ftc_domain_type}::
  1698→A
  1699→{ftc_form}
  1700→that wraps a
  1701→{ftc_primitive}
  1702→with domain-specific meaning while maintaining the underlying binary representation.
  1703→{ftc_domain_type_s}
  1704→enhance type safety by preventing accidental misuse of values (e.g., treating a Temperature as a Pressure) while imposing no runtime overhead.
  1705→
  1706→[[ftc_enumeration]]
  1707→{ftc_enumeration}::
  1708→A
  1709→{ftc_form}
  1710→representing a closed set of named symbolic
  1711→{ftc_value_s}
  1712→that map to sequential integers starting from zero.
  1713→{ftc_enumeration_s}
  1714→provide compile-time exhaustiveness checking and clear state representation.
  1715→When used as a
  1716→{smc_posture},
  1717→an
  1718→{ftc_enumeration}
  1719→becomes the primary state indicator for visualizing
  1720→{smc_ward}
  1721→behavior.
  1722→
  1723→[[ftc_value]]
  1724→{ftc_value}::
  1725→A named constant within an
  1726→{ftc_enumeration}.
  1727→Each
  1728→{ftc_value}
  1729→maps to a unique sequential integer within its containing
  1730→{ftc_enumeration}.
  1731→
  1732→[[ftc_record]]
  1733→{ftc_record}::
  1734→A
  1735→{ftc_form}
  1736→that groups heterogeneous
  1737→{ftc_member_s}
  1738→into a single reusable structure with defined field ordering and alignment.
  1739→{ftc_record_s}
  1740→support nesting (records containing other records), arrays of any
  1741→{ftc_form},
  1742→and maintain consistent binary layout across language backends.
  1743→Named
  1744→{ftc_record_s}
  1745→promote type reuse, while anonymous records enable inline structure definition.
  1746→
  1747→[[ftc_member]]
  1748→{ftc_member}::
  1749→A named, typed component within a
  1750→{ftc_record}.
  1751→Each
  1752→{ftc_member}
  1753→has a specific
  1754→{ftc_form}
  1755→and occupies a defined position in the
  1756→{ftc_record_s}
  1757→memory layout.
  1758→
  1759→[[ftc_array]]
  1760→{ftc_array}::
  1761→A
  1762→{ftc_form}
  1763→containing elements of a single type with size determined by a
  1764→{ftc_bound}.
  1765→Runtime length varies from zero to the bound's limit for dynamic arrays, or remains constant for fixed arrays.
  1766→
  1767→[[ftc_coarray]]
  1768→{ftc_coarray}::
  1769→A composite structure defining multiple arrays that share a common dynamic size.
  1770→All arrays within a
  1771→{ftc_coarray}
  1772→are accessed using the same index values and maintain synchronized lengths.
  1773→A
  1774→{ftc_coarray}
  1775→includes an implicit size property tracking the current element count.
  1776→
  1777→[[ftc_index]]
  1778→{ftc_index}::
  1779→A
  1780→{ftc_domain_type}
  1781→created by a
  1782→{ftc_bound}
  1783→that provides type-safe array access.
  1784→{ftc_index}
  1785→values are constrained to valid positions within arrays created from the same bound, enabling bounds-checked access in supported language backends.
  1786→
  1787→[[smc_alliance]]
  1788→{smc_alliance}::
  1789→The complete runtime instantiation of a potentially distributed system created from exactly one
  1790→{smc_shape}
  1791→specification.
  1792→The
  1793→{smc_alliance}
  1794→encompasses all running
  1795→{pmp_realm_s}, {smc_fief_s}, and {smc_ward_s}
  1796→as they execute and interact according to the architectural blueprint defined by its
  1797→{smc_shape}.
  1798→A
  1799→{smc_alliance}
  1800→exists within a
  1801→{smc_fixture_s}
  1802→selected by the
  1803→{smc_forge}
  1804→based on testing or deployment requirements.
  1805→
  1806→[[smc_shape]]
  1807→{smc_shape}::
  1808→A deployment specification defining the complete topology and parameterized resource allocation for a
  1809→{smc_alliance},
  1810→including: the number and placement of
  1811→{pmp_realm_s},
  1812→the
  1813→{smc_topology}
  1814→pattern and
  1815→{smc_transport}
  1816→mechanisms,
  1817→{smc_constant}
  1818→declarations, preallocated
  1819→{smc_fief}
  1820→slots, queue depths, timer limits, and
  1821→{smc_hearth}
  1822→requirements.
  1823→Each
  1824→{smc_shape}
  1825→serves as a template requiring
  1826→{smc_constant}
  1827→values from
  1828→{smc_forge}
  1829→instantiations to produce executable code.
  1830→The
  1831→{smc_shape}
  1832→generates multiple
  1833→{smc_fixture_s}
  1834→each supporting different execution combinations.
  1835→For testing,
  1836→{smc_shape_s}
  1837→enable
  1838→{toc_regent_ward}, {toc_bailiff_ward}, {toc_interceptable_ward},
  1839→and
  1840→{toc_synthetic_fief}
  1841→inclusions to provide test control when natural management
  1842→{smc_ward_s}
  1843→are absent.
  1844→
  1845→[[smc_forge]]
  1846→{smc_forge}::
  1847→A comprehensive test and deployment orchestration framework that instantiates
  1848→{smc_shape_s}
  1849→through their generated
  1850→{smc_fixture_s},
  1851→selecting execution model and
  1852→{smc_time_source}
  1853→based on testing or deployment requirements.
  1854→
  1855→[[smc_fixture]]
  1856→{smc_fixture}::
  1857→A runtime controller class generated from a
  1858→{smc_shape}
  1859→that executes a
  1860→{smc_alliance}
  1861→according to a specific combination of
  1862→{smc_time_source}, {smc_topology}, {smc_transport}
  1863→mechanisms, and threading model.
  1864→Each
  1865→{smc_shape}
  1866→generates multiple
  1867→{smc_fixture}
  1868→classes implementing different execution models: virtual or real
  1869→{smc_time_source},
  1870→single or multi-threaded
  1871→{pmp_realm}
  1872→execution, and support for the
  1873→{smc_shape_p}
  1874→specific
  1875→{smc_topology}
  1876→pattern.
  1877→The
  1878→{smc_fixture}
  1879→inherits
  1880→{smc_topology}
  1881→and
  1882→{smc_transport}
  1883→specifications from its generating
  1884→{smc_shape}
  1885→while adding execution-specific parameters.
  1886→All
  1887→{smc_fixture_s}
  1888→from a
  1889→{smc_shape}
  1890→expose uniform
  1891→{toc_directive_s}
  1892→enabling
  1893→{smc_forge_case_s}
  1894→to execute across deterministic single-threaded
  1895→testing with virtual
  1896→{smc_time_source},
  1897→concurrent testing with controlled
  1898→{smc_timebase},
  1899→or real-time production deployment.
  1900→The
  1901→{smc_forge}
  1902→selects
  1903→appropriate
  1904→{smc_fixture_s}
  1905→based on testing or deployment requirements.
  1906→Example
  1907→{smc_fixture_s}
  1908→include `VirtualMonolithicRealm`, `VirtualDistributedRealm`,
  1909→and `ProductionRealm`.
  1910→
  1911→[[smc_forge_case]]
  1912→{smc_forge_case}::
  1913→A test case that can execute against multiple
  1914→{smc_fixture_s}
  1915→generated from related
  1916→{smc_shape_s},
  1917→enabling comprehensive testing across different deployment configurations
  1918→without test duplication.
  1919→
  1920→[[smc_gerent]]
  1921→{smc_gerent}::
  1922→A
  1923→{smc_ward}
  1924→empowered to allocate and destroy
  1925→{smc_fief}
  1926→instances at runtime using
  1927→{gcm_levy_s}
  1928→and
  1929→{gcm_dissolver_s},
  1930→maintaining
  1931→{smc_shadow_s}
  1932→for
  1933→{smc_ward_s}
  1934→within its managed
  1935→{smc_fief_s}.
  1936→Each
  1937→{smc_gerent}
  1938→can target any connected
  1939→{pmp_realm}
  1940→within the
  1941→{smc_alliance}
  1942→for
  1943→{smc_fief}
  1944→allocation, with the ultimate determination of placement made by
  1945→{pmp_business_context}
  1946→logic.
  1947→The
  1948→{smc_gerent}
  1949→must respect any
  1950→{smc_locality_s}
  1951→specified in the
  1952→{smc_fief}
  1953→type, selecting target
  1954→{pmp_realm_s}
  1955→and
  1956→{smc_steward}
  1957→instances that satisfy all co-location requirements.
  1958→{smc_steward}
  1959→bindings are specified at
  1960→{smc_fief}
  1961→founding time and remain immutable throughout the
  1962→{smc_fief}
  1963→instance lifetime.
  1964→Each
  1965→{smc_gerent}
  1966→must reside in a different
  1967→{smc_fief}
  1968→type than those it manages, and the relationship between
  1969→{smc_gerent_s}
  1970→and their managed
  1971→{smc_fief_s}
  1972→is defined in the
  1973→{smc_accord}.
  1974→The
  1975→{smc_hearth}
  1976→is the sole exception, having no managing
  1977→{smc_gerent}.
  1978→
  1979→[[smc_locality]]
  1980→{smc_locality}::
  1981→A specification-time constraint that restricts the relative placement of
  1982→{smc_ward_s}
  1983→across
  1984→{pmp_realm}
  1985→boundaries to ensure required co-location for performance-critical communication paths.
  1986→Each
  1987→{smc_locality}
  1988→specifies one of three constraint levels: same-{pmp_realm}
  1989→(enforcing
  1990→{pmp_ledger}
  1991→communication), same-server (allowing different
  1992→{pmp_realm_s}
  1993→on one machine), or unconstrained (permitting full distribution).
  1994→{smc_locality_s}
  1995→govern two distinct placement relationships.
  1996→First,
  1997→{smc_fief}-internal
  1998→constraints specified during
  1999→{smc_fief}
  2000→definition restrict where the
  2001→{smc_fief_p} {smc_vassal_s}
  2002→execute relative to their managing
  2003→{smc_gerent}.
  2004→Second,
  2005→{smc_steward}
  2006→binding constraints specified on
  2007→{smc_steward}
  2008→requirements within a
  2009→{smc_fief}
  2010→restrict where that
  2011→{smc_fief}
  2012→can be allocated relative to its bound
  2013→{smc_steward_s}.
  2014→The
  2015→{smc_gerent}
  2016→must honor all applicable
  2017→{smc_locality_s}
  2018→when invoking
  2019→{gcm_levy},
  2020→selecting both a target
  2021→{pmp_realm}
  2022→and specific
  2023→{smc_steward}
  2024→instances that satisfy every constraint.
  2025→
  2026→[[smc_hearth]]
  2027→{smc_hearth}::
  2028→A special
  2029→{smc_fief}
  2030→type that is automatically instantiated when a
  2031→{pmp_realm}
  2032→starts, limited to exactly one instance per
  2033→{pmp_realm}.
  2034→The
  2035→{smc_hearth}
  2036→hosts business lifecycle manager
  2037→{smc_ward_s}
  2038→that bootstrap and manage the
  2039→{pmp_realm_p}
  2040→operational concerns.
  2041→Unlike other
  2042→{smc_fief_s},
  2043→it has no managing
  2044→{smc_gerent}
  2045→and shares the
  2046→{pmp_realm_p}
  2047→complete lifecycle.
  2048→The
  2049→{smc_shape}
  2050→determines which
  2051→{pmp_realm_s}
  2052→require a
  2053→{smc_hearth}.
  2054→
  2055→[[smc_property]]
  2056→{smc_property}::
  2057→Named state within a
  2058→{smc_ward}
  2059→that persists for the containing
  2060→{smc_fief_p}
  2061→lifetime.
  2062→It may contain primitives, domain types, arrays, or records.
  2063→
  2064→[[smc_posture]]
  2065→{smc_posture}::
  2066→An enumeration
  2067→{smc_property}
  2068→designated as the primary state indicator for a
  2069→{smc_ward}.
  2070→At most one
  2071→{smc_property}
  2072→per
  2073→{smc_ward}
  2074→may be marked as its
  2075→{smc_posture},
  2076→providing a standard way to represent and visualize
  2077→{smc_ward}
  2078→state machines while enabling potential runtime optimizations.
  2079→
  2080→[[smc_shadow]]
  2081→{smc_shadow}::
  2082→Runtime per-connection state instances created from
  2083→{smc_bond}
  2084→property templates when a
  2085→{smc_fief}
  2086→instance is created.
  2087→
  2088→[[gcm_handler]]
  2089→{gcm_handler}::
  2090→A developer-written method affiliated with a
  2091→{smc_ward}
  2092→that responds to events in
  2093→{pmp_transport_context}.
  2094→Each
  2095→{gcm_handler}
  2096→has an interface derived from the
  2097→{smc_accord}
  2098→but is implemented in
  2099→{pmp_business_context}.
  2100→The
  2101→{smc_accord}
  2102→specifies which
  2103→{gcm_handler_s}
  2104→are required for each
  2105→{smc_ward}
  2106→type.
  2107→+
  2108→{gcm_handler_s}
  2109→include:
  2110→+
  2111→* {mcd_message}
  2112→handling:
  2113→{hdm_receiver}, {gcm_glean}
  2114→* Time events:
  2115→{hdm_timeout}
  2116→* Lifecycle events:
  2117→{hdm_init}, {hdm_lodge}, {hdm_vacate}
  2118→* {mcd_summons} completion: {gcm_thresh}
  2119→
  2120→[[gcm_utility]]
  2121→{gcm_utility}::
  2122→A generated method defined and implemented in
  2123→{pmp_transport_context}
  2124→that
  2125→{smc_ward_s}
  2126→invoke during
  2127→{gcm_handler_s}.
  2128→Each
  2129→{gcm_utility}
  2130→is available only to specific
  2131→{smc_ward}
  2132→types as determined by the
  2133→{smc_accord}.
  2134→+
  2135→{gcm_utility_s}
  2136→include:
  2137→+
  2138→* Communication:
  2139→{gcm_sender}
  2140→* {smc_fief}
  2141→management:
  2142→{gcm_levy}, {gcm_dissolver}, {gcm_survey}, {gcm_scout}
  2143→* Error reporting:
  2144→{gcm_alert}, {gcm_panic}
  2145→
  2146→== {gcm_handler_s} and {gcm_utility_s}
  2147→
  2148→[[gcm_alert]]
  2149→{gcm_alert}::
  2150→A
  2151→{gcm_utility}
  2152→that
  2153→{smc_ward_s}
  2154→invoke to report failures that do not threaten system integrity.
  2155→
  2156→[[gcm_panic]]
  2157→{gcm_panic}::
  2158→A
  2159→{gcm_utility}
  2160→that
  2161→{smc_ward_s}
  2162→invoke to report detection of critical system integrity failure that typically results in catastrophic exit.
  2163→
  2164→[[gcm_levy]]
  2165→{gcm_levy}::
  2166→A
  2167→{gcm_utility}
  2168→that
  2169→{smc_gerent_s}
  2170→invoke to allocate new
  2171→{smc_fief}
  2172→instances in local or remote {pmp_realm_s}.
  2173→Each
  2174→{gcm_levy}
  2175→handles the transport machinery for
  2176→{smc_fief}
  2177→creation and
  2178→{smc_steward}
  2179→binding across the distributed system.
  2180→
  2181→[[gcm_dissolver]]
  2182→{gcm_dissolver}::
  2183→A
  2184→{gcm_utility}
  2185→that
  2186→{smc_gerent_s}
  2187→invoke to destroy existing
  2188→{smc_fief}
  2189→instances in local or remote
  2190→{pmp_realm_s}.
  2191→Each
  2192→{gcm_dissolver}
  2193→handles the transport machinery for
  2194→{smc_fief}
  2195→destruction and
  2196→{smc_steward}
  2197→unbinding notifications across the distributed system.
  2198→
  2199→[[gcm_survey]]
  2200→{gcm_survey}::
  2201→A
  2202→{gcm_utility}
  2203→that
  2204→{smc_gerent_s}
  2205→invoke to enumerate available
  2206→{smc_steward}
  2207→instances suitable for binding to a new
  2208→{smc_fief}.
  2209→{gcm_survey}
  2210→returns a read-only collection of
  2211→{smc_steward}
  2212→candidates valid for use given where the new
  2213→{smc_fief}
  2214→instance will be created upon call to the
  2215→{gcm_levy}.
  2216→
  2217→[[gcm_scout]]
  2218→{gcm_scout}::
  2219→A
  2220→{gcm_utility}
  2221→that
  2222→{smc_gerent_s}
  2223→invoke to enumerate available
  2224→{pmp_realm_s}
  2225→within the
  2226→{smc_alliance}
  2227→where the
  2228→{smc_gerent}
  2229→can create corresponding
  2230→{smc_fief}
  2231→instances.
  2232→
  2233→[[gcm_sender]]
  2234→{gcm_sender}::
  2235→A
  2236→{gcm_utility}
  2237→that
  2238→{smc_ward_s}
  2239→invoke to create and transmit a specific
  2240→{mcd_message}
  2241→to another
  2242→{smc_ward}.
  2243→Each
  2244→{gcm_sender}
  2245→handles serialization into the
  2246→{pmp_binary_format}
  2247→and message routing, appearing identical to
  2248→{smc_ward}
  2249→implementers regardless of whether the target is a
  2250→{smc_steward}
  2251→or peer
  2252→{smc_ward}
  2253→within the same
  2254→{smc_fief}
  2255→instance.
  2256→
  2257→[[hdm_init]]
  2258→{hdm_init}::
  2259→A
  2260→{gcm_handler}
  2261→that responds to
  2262→{smc_fief}
  2263→instance construction.
  2264→{hdm_init_s}
  2265→enable
  2266→{smc_ward_s}
  2267→to establish initial state, arm
  2268→{smc_timer_s},
  2269→or trigger bootstrapping sequences.
  2270→{smc_hearth} {smc_ward_s}
  2271→use
  2272→{hdm_init_s}
  2273→to begin sophisticated construction processes upon
  2274→{pmp_realm}
  2275→startup.
  2276→
  2277→[[hdm_receiver]]
  2278→{hdm_receiver}::
  2279→A
  2280→{gcm_handler}
  2281→that responds to a specific incoming
  2282→{mcd_message}
  2283→within a
  2284→{smc_ward}.
  2285→Each
  2286→{hdm_receiver}
  2287→manipulates
  2288→{smc_ward}
  2289→state and potentially invokes {gcm_utility_s} in response.
  2290→
  2291→[[hdm_timeout]]
  2292→{hdm_timeout}::
  2293→A
  2294→{gcm_handler}
  2295→that responds to
  2296→{smc_timer}
  2297→expiration events.
  2298→{hdm_timeout_s}
  2299→are processed sequentially with other message events and may invoke
  2300→{gcm_utility_s}
  2301→in response.
  2302→Each named
  2303→{smc_timer}
  2304→triggers its own distinct
  2305→{hdm_timeout}
  2306→handler in
  2307→{pmp_business_context}.
  2308→
  2309→[[hdm_vacate]]
  2310→{hdm_vacate}::
  2311→A
  2312→{gcm_handler}
  2313→that
  2314→{smc_steward_s}
  2315→implement to respond to notification when a bound
  2316→{smc_fief}
  2317→instance has been destroyed.
  2318→{hdm_vacate_s}
  2319→enable
  2320→{smc_steward_s}
  2321→to clean up resources and update their state, receiving a
  2322→{smc_shadow}
  2323→context if the
  2324→{smc_steward}
  2325→has fostered a
  2326→{smc_bond}
  2327→type for the disconnecting
  2328→{smc_tenant}
  2329→type.
  2330→
  2331→[[hdm_lodge]]
  2332→{hdm_lodge}::
  2333→A
  2334→{gcm_handler}
  2335→that
  2336→{smc_steward_s}
  2337→implement to respond to notification when the {smc_steward} has been bound to a new
  2338→{smc_fief}
  2339→instance.
  2340→{hdm_lodge_s}
  2341→enable
  2342→{smc_steward_s}
  2343→to react to new organizational relationships and receive a
  2344→{smc_shadow}
  2345→context if the
  2346→{smc_steward}
  2347→has
  2348→{cbi_foster_ed} a {smc_bond}
  2349→type for the connecting
  2350→{smc_tenant}
  2351→type.
  2352→
  2353→[[toc_regent_ward]]
  2354→{toc_regent_ward}::
  2355→A test interface generated for absent
  2356→{smc_gerent_s}
  2357→that provides
  2358→{toc_regent_levy}, {toc_regent_dissolve},
  2359→and
  2360→{toc_regent_send}

  2362→{smc_fief_s}.
  2363→
  2364→[[toc_bailiff_ward]]
  2365→{toc_bailiff_ward}::
  2366→A test interface generated for absent
  2367→{smc_steward_s}

  2369→{smc_shadow_s}

  2371→{smc_tenant_s}

  2373→{toc_bailiff_bind},
  2374→{toc_bailiff_send},

  2376→{noc_bailiff_receive}

  2378→
  2379→[[toc_missive]]
  2380→{toc_missive}::
  2381→A test-side representation of a
  2382→{mcd_message}

  2385→operations.
  2386→
  2387→[[toc_regent_levy]]
  2388→{toc_regent_levy}::
  2389→A
  2390→{toc_directive}
  2391→that allocates a new
  2392→{smc_fief}
  2393→instance through a
  2394→{toc_regent_ward}

  2396→{pmp_realm}
  2397→and
  2398→{smc_steward}
  2399→binding specifications.
  2400→
  2401→[[toc_regent_dissolve]]
  2402→{toc_regent_dissolve}::
  2403→A
  2404→{toc_directive}
  2405→that destroys an existing
  2406→{smc_fief}

  2410→[[toc_regent_send]]

  2413→{toc_directive}

  2415→{toc_missive}

  2417→{toc_regent_ward}

  2419→{smc_vassal_s}

  2421→{smc_fief_s}.

  2423→[[toc_bailiff_bind]]

  2426→{toc_directive}

  2428→{smc_tenant}

  2432→{smc_steward}.

  2434→[[toc_bailiff_send]]
  2435→{toc_bailiff_send}::

  2437→{toc_directive}
  2438→that transmits a
  2439→{toc_missive}

  2441→{toc_bailiff_ward}

  2443→{smc_tenant}.
  2444→
  2445→[[noc_regent_allocation]]
  2446→{noc_regent_allocation}::

  2448→{noc_notice}

  2450→{smc_fief}

  2454→[[noc_bailiff_receive]]
  2455→{noc_bailiff_receive}::

  2457→{noc_notice}

  2459→{smc_tenant}

  2463→{toc_bailiff_ward}.

  2465→[[noc_bailiff_lodge]]

  2468→{noc_notice}

  2472→{smc_tenant_s}

  2476→{smc_steward}.
  2477→
  2478→[[smc_constant]]

  2488→{smc_fief}

  2491→{smc_shape_s}

  2493→{smc_constant}.

  2495→[[toc_synthetic_fief]]
  2496→{toc_synthetic_fief}::
  2497→A
  2498→{smc_fief}
  2499→marked for complete test replacement where test code provides mock implementations of all
  2500→{smc_ward}
  2501→behaviors while accessing original
  2502→{smc_property}
  2503→definitions.
  2504→
  2505→[[toc_interceptable_ward]]
  2506→{toc_interceptable_ward}::
  2507→A
  2508→{smc_ward}
  2509→type marked for test observation and control where all
  2510→{mcd_message_s}
  2511→and
  2512→{smc_timer}
  2513→expirations route through a test-provided
  2514→{toc_ward_interceptor}.
  2515→
  2516→[[toc_ward_interceptor]]
  2517→{toc_ward_interceptor}::
  2518→A test-provided class that receives all events for an
  2519→{toc_interceptable_ward}
  2520→type and controls per-instance routing through
  2521→{toc_supplant}.
  2522→
  2523→[[toc_intervention]]
  2524→{toc_intervention}::
  2525→An operation available within
  2526→{noc_notice}
  2527→callback contexts that modifies
  2528→{smc_ward}
  2529→state or controls execution flow during
  2530→{smc_fixture}
  2531→processing.
  2532→
  2533→[[toc_supplant]]
  2534→{toc_supplant}::
  2535→An
  2536→{toc_intervention}
  2537→that configures message routing for a specific
  2538→{smc_ward}
  2539→instance within a
  2540→{toc_ward_interceptor}.
  2541→
  2542→[[toc_inspect]]
  2543→{toc_inspect}::
  2544→A testing operation that provides read-only access to
  2545→{smc_ward} {smc_property_s}
  2546→between
  2547→{pmp_executive_cycle_s}.
  2548→
  2549→[[toc_probe]]
  2550→{toc_probe}::
  2551→A testing operation that provides read-only access to
  2552→{smc_shadow}
  2553→state between
  2554→{pmp_executive_cycle_s}.
  2555→
  2556→[[toc_inject]]
  2557→{toc_inject}::
  2558→A testing operation that places synthetic
  2559→{mcd_message_s}
  2560→into
  2561→{pmp_realm} {pmp_ingress_queue_s}.
  2562→
  2563→[[toc_directive]]
  2564→{toc_directive}::
  2565→An atomic operation invoked on a
  2566→{smc_fixture}
  2567→to control execution and system state within the testing environment.
  2568→
  2569→[[noc_notice]]
  2570→{noc_notice}::
  2571→An event callback from a
  2572→{smc_fixture}
  2573→to the
  2574→{smc_forge}
  2575→reporting significant occurrences during
  2576→{pmp_executive_cycle}
  2577→processing.
  2578→
  2579→[[smc_timebase]]
  2580→{smc_timebase}::
  2581→The virtual time counter maintained by virtual time
  2582→{smc_fixture}
  2583→that advances only through explicit
  2584→{toc_advance}
  2585→or
  2586→{toc_next_timeout}
  2587→directives.
  2588→The
  2589→{smc_timebase}
  2590→governs all
  2591→{smc_timer}
  2592→expiration calculations within virtual execution environments, enabling deterministic reproduction of time-dependent behaviors.
  2593→
  2594→[[smc_realm_id]]
  2595→{smc_realm_id}::
  2596→A runtime-unique identifier assigned to each
  2597→{pmp_realm}
  2598→instance within a
  2599→{smc_alliance}.
  2600→The
  2601→{smc_realm_id}
  2602→enables unambiguous message routing and
  2603→{smc_ward}
  2604→location tracking across the distributed system.
  2605→
  2606→[[smc_fief_id]]
  2607→{smc_fief_id}::
  2608→A runtime-unique identifier assigned to each
  2609→{smc_fief}
  2610→instance at allocation time.
  2611→The
  2612→{smc_fief_id}
  2613→combined with
  2614→{smc_realm_id}
  2615→provides complete addressing context for
  2616→{smc_ward}
  2617→instances within the
  2618→{smc_alliance}.
  2619→
  2620→[[smc_ward_id]]
  2621→{smc_ward_id}::
  2622→A runtime-unique identifier within a
  2623→{smc_fief}
  2624→instance that distinguishes individual
  2625→{smc_ward_s}
  2626→of different types.
  2627→The complete address tuple of
  2628→{smc_realm_id}, {smc_fief_id},
  2629→and
  2630→{smc_ward_id}
  2631→uniquely identifies any
  2632→{smc_ward}
  2633→instance for message routing.
  2634→
  2635→[[toc_advance]]
  2636→{toc_advance}::
  2637→A
  2638→{smc_fixture} {toc_directive}
  2639→that progresses time by a specified duration and executes all
  2640→{smc_timer}
  2641→expirations within that period.
  2642→The specific behavior with virtual versus real
  2643→{smc_time_source_s}
  2644→is described in
  2645→{smc_architecture_patterns}.
  2646→
  2647→[[toc_next_timeout]]
  2648→{toc_next_timeout}::
  2649→A
  2650→{smc_fixture} {toc_directive}
  2651→that advances to the next pending
  2652→{smc_timer}
  2653→expiration and returns the new
  2654→{smc_timebase}
  2655→value.
  2656→
  2657→[[toc_query]]
  2658→{toc_query}::
  2659→A
  2660→{smc_fixture} {toc_directive}
  2661→that retrieves current
  2662→{smc_property}
  2663→values for a specific
  2664→{smc_ward}
  2665→instance.
  2666→
  2667→[[toc_found]]
  2668→{toc_found}::
  2669→A
  2670→{smc_fixture} {toc_directive}
  2671→that creates a new
  2672→{smc_fief}
  2673→instance within the controlled environment.
  2674→
  2675→[[toc_destroy]]
  2676→{toc_destroy}::
  2677→A
  2678→{smc_fixture} {toc_directive}
  2679→that removes an existing
  2680→{smc_fief}
  2681→instance from the controlled environment.
  2682→
  2683→[[noc_message_queued]]
  2684→{noc_message_queued}::
  2685→A
  2686→{smc_fixture} {noc_notice}
  2687→generated when a
  2688→{smc_ward}
  2689→places a
  2690→{mcd_message}
  2691→onto the
  2692→{pmp_ledger}.
  2693→
  2694→[[noc_message_dispatched]]
  2695→{noc_message_dispatched}::
  2696→A
  2697→{smc_fixture} {noc_notice}
  2698→generated when
  2699→{gcm_dispatch}
  2700→delivers a
  2701→{mcd_message}
  2702→to its target
  2703→{smc_ward}.
  2704→
  2705→[[noc_message_emitted]]
  2706→{noc_message_emitted}::
  2707→A
  2708→{smc_fixture} {noc_notice}
  2709→generated when a
  2710→{smc_ward}
  2711→sends a
  2712→{mcd_message}
  2713→destined for a different
  2714→{pmp_realm}.
  2715→
  2716→[[noc_timer_expired]]
  2717→{noc_timer_expired}::
  2718→A
  2719→{smc_fixture} {noc_notice}
  2720→generated when a
  2721→{smc_timer}
  2722→triggers its associated
  2723→{hdm_timeout}
  2724→handler.
  2725→
  2726→[[smc_architecture_patterns]]
  2727→{smc_architecture_patterns}::
  2728→The compositional relationships between
  2729→{smc_topology},
  2730→{smc_transport},
  2731→and
  2732→{smc_time_source}
  2733→that govern
  2734→{smc_alliance}
  2735→behavior across different deployment configurations.
  2736→
  2737→[[smc_topology]]
  2738→{smc_topology}::
  2739→A
  2740→{smc_shape}-declared
  2741→pattern defining the logical structure of
  2742→inter-{pmp_realm}
  2743→connections within a
  2744→{smc_alliance}.
  2745→Each
  2746→{smc_topology}
  2747→specifies message flow patterns independent of
  2748→{smc_transport}
  2749→implementation as described in
  2750→{smc_architecture_patterns}.
  2751→
  2752→[[smc_topology_monolithic]]
  2753→{smc_topology_monolithic}::
  2754→A degenerate
  2755→{smc_topology}
  2756→pattern containing exactly one
  2757→{pmp_realm}
  2758→with no
  2759→inter-{pmp_realm} {mcd_message_ing}.
  2760→This pattern serves early development, simple systems, testing, and simulation scenarios where distribution would obscure
  2761→{pmp_business_context}
  2762→behavior.
  2763→
  2764→[[smc_topology_paired]]
  2765→{smc_topology_paired}::
  2766→A
  2767→{smc_topology}
  2768→pattern connecting exactly two
  2769→{pmp_realm_s}
  2770→through bidirectional message channels.
  2771→This pattern models client-server relationships, producer-consumer pipelines, and other naturally bilateral architectures.
  2772→
  2773→[[smc_topology_switchboard]]
  2774→{smc_topology_switchboard}::
  2775→A star
  2776→{smc_topology}
  2777→pattern where multiple
  2778→{pmp_realm_s}
  2779→connect through a central
  2780→{pmp_switchboard}
  2781→that performs high-speed message routing.
  2782→This pattern enables many-to-many
  2783→{smc_ward}
  2784→communication while maintaining single-writer queue disciplines.
  2785→
  2786→[[pmp_switchboard]]
  2787→{pmp_switchboard}::
  2788→A dedicated execution context in
  2789→{smc_topology_switchboard}
  2790→deployments that routes
  2791→{mcd_message_s}
  2792→between
  2793→{pmp_realm_s}
  2794→based on
  2795→{smc_ward}
  2796→addresses.
  2797→The
  2798→{pmp_switchboard}
  2799→hosts no
  2800→{smc_ward_s}
  2801→and examines only message headers to maintain transport-level routing without coupling to
  2802→{smc_accord}
  2803→semantics.
  2804→
  2805→[[pmp_ledger]]
  2806→{pmp_ledger}::
  2807→The message buffer within a
  2808→{pmp_realm}
  2809→that records both pending
  2810→{mcd_message_s}
  2811→for execution ordering and
  2812→{pmp_watch}-captured
  2813→field values from
  2814→{pmp_elision}-optimized
  2815→calls.
  2816→Replaces the former
  2817→{pmp_ledger}
  2818→concept with expanded trace collection responsibilities.
  2819→
  2820→[[pmp_elision]]
  2821→{pmp_elision}::
  2822→An optimization where
  2823→{gcm_sender}
  2824→invocations to
  2825→{smc_ward_s}
  2826→within the same
  2827→{pmp_realm}
  2828→directly invoke the target
  2829→{hdm_receiver}
  2830→without serialization, provided no handler is currently on the target
  2831→{smc_ward_p}
  2832→stack.
  2833→Elided messages bypass
  2834→{pmp_binary_format}
  2835→serialization but may still record watched fields to the
  2836→{pmp_ledger}
  2837→for trace visibility.
  2838→
  2839→[[pmp_watch]]
  2840→{pmp_watch}::
  2841→A specification-time debugging rule that identifies specific
  2842→{mcd_field_s}
  2843→to capture in the
  2844→{pmp_ledger}
  2845→even when their containing
  2846→{mcd_message}
  2847→is elided.
  2848→{pmp_watch}
  2849→rules ensure critical state transitions remain visible in system traces without forcing full message serialization.
  2850→
  2851→[[smc_transport]]
  2852→{smc_transport}::
  2853→A
  2854→{smc_shape}-declared
  2855→mechanism specifying how
  2856→{mcd_message_s}
  2857→physically move between
  2858→{pmp_realm_s}
  2859→within
  2860→{smc_topology}
  2861→constraints.
  2862→Each implements
  2863→{pmp_binary_format}
  2864→delivery with different performance characteristics as described in
  2865→{smc_architecture_patterns}.
  2866→
  2867→[[smc_transport_inprocess]]
  2868→{smc_transport_inprocess}::
  2869→A zero-copy
  2870→{smc_transport}
  2871→mechanism for
  2872→{pmp_realm_s}
  2873→within the same process, implementing delivery through direct memory access.
  2874→
  2875→[[smc_transport_sharedmemory]]
  2876→{smc_transport_sharedmemory}::
  2877→A high-performance
  2878→{smc_transport}
  2879→using lock-free circular buffers for
  2880→{pmp_realm_s}
  2881→in different processes on the same machine.
  2882→
  2883→[[smc_transport_localhostudp]]
  2884→{smc_transport_localhostudp}::
  2885→A pragmatic
  2886→{smc_transport}
  2887→using UDP sockets over localhost, delegating queueing and flow control to the OS network stack.
  2888→
  2889→[[smc_transport_networkudp]]
  2890→{smc_transport_networkudp}::
  2891→A
  2892→{smc_transport}
  2893→extending UDP delivery across network boundaries,
  2894→handling fragmentation for payloads exceeding MTU limits.
  2895→
  2896→[[smc_time_source]]
  2897→{smc_time_source}::
  2898→The mechanism governing temporal progression within a
  2899→{smc_alliance},
  2900→selected by the
  2901→{smc_forge}
  2902→at instantiation as described in
  2903→{smc_architecture_patterns}.
  2904→
  2905→[[pmp_egress_queue]]
  2906→{pmp_egress_queue}::
  2907→The outbound
  2908→{mcd_message}
  2909→buffer within each
  2910→{pmp_realm}
  2911→that receives the complete
  2912→{pmp_ledger}
  2913→contents at
  2914→{pmp_executive_cycle}
  2915→completion.
  2916→In
  2917→{smc_topology_switchboard}
  2918→deployments, the
  2919→{pmp_egress_queue}
  2920→decouples
  2921→{smc_ward}
  2922→execution from
  2923→{pmp_switchboard}
  2924→processing through single-writer semantics while providing full trace visibility.
  2925→
  2926→[[csc_mcm_metaconceptmodel]]
  2927→{csc_mcm_metaconceptmodel}::
  2928→The foundational document (`mcm-MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc`) that defines the documentation patterns, structures, and curation actions used to construct and maintain
  2929→{smc_wardrealmsubstrate}
  2930→Model concept models.
  2931→This meta-document establishes the rules for
  2932→{csc_mcm_linked_term_s},
  2933→their Definition organization,
  2934→{csc_mcm_task_lens}
  2935→specifications, and document evolution patterns that govern all CSX documentation.
  2936→
  2937→[[csc_mcm_linked_term]]
  2938→{csc_mcm_linked_term}::
  2939→A formal reference mechanism that creates navigable connections between AsciiDoc attribute declarations and their corresponding anchored definitions.
  2940→Each
  2941→{csc_mcm_linked_term}
  2942→consists of an attribute reference (e.g., `:smc_ward:`) that maps to a replacement text containing both display form and anchor target (e.g., `<<smc_ward,Cell>>`).
  2943→The
  2944→{csc_mcm_mapping_section}
  2945→contains all such mappings, enabling consistent terminology throughout the document.
  2946→See
  2947→{csc_mcm_metaconceptmodel}
  2948→for complete patterns and variant rules.
  2949→
  2950→[[csc_mcm_mapping_section]]
  2951→{csc_mcm_mapping_section}::
  2952→The strictly formatted document section containing all attribute-to-anchor mappings that implement
  2953→{csc_mcm_linked_term}
  2954→references.
  2955→Located between `// tag::mapping-section[]` and `// end::mapping-section[]` markers, this section enforces alphabetical ordering, column alignment, and category organization.
  2956→See
  2957→{csc_mcm_metaconceptmodel}
  2958→for formatting constraints and curation rules.
  2959→
  2960→[[csc_mcm_task_lens]]
  2961→{csc_mcm_task_lens}::
  2962→A structured specification that defines focused activation patterns within a concept model, emphasizing specific terms and relationships relevant to particular work contexts.
  2963→Each
  2964→{csc_mcm_task_lens}
  2965→identifies for Claude-as-opus-4-model a curated view suitable for carrying out a category of tasks.
  2966→When compiled, a
  2967→{csc_mcm_task_lens}
  2968→produces a claudex representation.
  2969→See
  2970→{csc_tlg_task_lens_guide}
  2971→for compilation specifications and usage patterns.
  2972→
  2973→[[csc_tlg_task_lens_guide]]
  2974→{csc_tlg_task_lens_guide}::
  2975→The operational document (`csx-TLG-TaskLensGuide.md`) that specifies how to compile task-focused representations from
  2976→{smc_wardrealmsubstrate}
  2977→Model concept definitions.
  2978→The
  2979→{csc_tlg_task_lens_guide}
  2980→defines lens structure patterns, compilation mechanics, and focal depth activation rules for creating work-optimized views of concept constellations.
